commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=12509d35bd00e59236d2cea2e6196eff354ff693)
commands.review_summary:
The PR clears process-current provider-auth warm state during universal CLI teardown and adds coverage for terminating an active synthetic auth warm worker after local JSON completion.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +60. Total +71 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at report level: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/92464 provides a concrete scale-sensitive local-memory workload. This review did not rerun the 10,000-entry environment or capture the retaining handle, so it is not a high-confidence current-main reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The contributor or a maintainer must identify the actual retained resource before choosing among memory, harness, and embedded-runner lifecycle owners; there is not yet a safe mechanical repair.

target.title: fix(cli): clear provider auth warm state on exit
target.body:
## Summary

- Clear process-current provider auth warm state during short-lived CLI teardown.
- Terminate any active provider-auth warm worker after local JSON agent completion instead of leaving cleanup to process shutdown.
- Add focused CLI exit coverage for `openclaw agent --local --json`.

Fixes #92464

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `openclaw agent --local --json` should return final JSON and naturally release short-lived CLI lifecycle resources, including process-current provider-auth warm state and any tracked provider-auth warm worker cleanup, instead of leaving teardown to process shutdown.

**Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `bfe2bdbb`. The agent turn used production CLI code with an isolated `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`/`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and a loopback vLLM-compatible stub server; no real provider credentials or external model calls were used.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# Temporary config pointed vllm/proof-model at http://127.0.0.1:<port>/v1
# with VLLM_API_KEY=vllm-local-proof and a loopback stub for /v1/chat/completions.
timeout 90s node scripts/run-node.mjs agent   --local   --json   --agent main   --session-key agent:main:proof-92464-worker-trace   --message 'Return exactly proof ok.'   --model vllm/proof-model

corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts
git diff --check
```

**Evidence after fix:** Successful local JSON turn with a real streamed model request to the loopback OpenAI-compatible stub, natural exit before timeout, and an after-exit process scan for the proof session and provider-auth worker command lines:

```text
EXIT_CODE=0
EXIT_SIGNAL=
ELAPSED_MS=29868
TIMEOUT_FIRED=no
STUB_REQUESTS=[{"method":"POST","url":"/v1/chat/completions","bodyBytes":103008,"stream":true}]
SURVIVORS=[]

STDOUT_EXCERPT={
  "payloads": [
    {
      "text": "proof ok",
      "mediaUrl": null
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "durationMs": 19005,
    "agentMeta": {
      "sessionId": "679b72fe-4602-4157-b9ae-a63f5e1847ff",
      "sessionFile": "/tmp/openclaw-92464-run-IvxHIG/state/agents/main/sessions/679b72fe-4602-4157-b9ae-a63f5e1847ff.jsonl",
      "provider": "vllm",
      "model": "proof-model",
      "contextTokens": 200000,
      "agentHarnessId": "openclaw",
      "usage": { "input": 1200, "output": 2, "total": 1202 },
      "lastCallUsage": { "input": 1200, "output": 2, "cacheRead": 0, "cacheWrite": 0, "total": 1202 }
    },
    "finalPromptText": "Return exactly proof ok.",
    "finalAssistantVisibleText": "proof ok",
    "finalAssistantRawText": "proof ok",
    "livenessState": "working",
    "stopReason": "stop",
    "executionTrace": {
      "winnerProvider": "vllm",
      "winnerModel": "proof-model",
      "attempts": [{ "provider": "vllm", "model": "proof-model", "result": "success", "stage": "assistant" }],
      "fallbackUsed": false,
      "runner": "embedded"
    },
    "completion": { "stopReason": "stop", "finishReason": "stop" }
  }
}

STDERR_EXCERPT=[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=9c81eceb-d688-4747-869c-8ff3f79cb234 sessionId=679b72fe-4602-4157-b9ae-a63f5e1847ff phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=14826 stages=workspace:2ms@2ms,runtime-plugins:14457ms@14459ms,hooks:2ms@14461ms,model-resolution:311ms@14772ms,auth:27ms@14799ms,context-engine:5ms@14804ms,attempt-workspace:20ms@14824ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@14824ms,attempt-runtime-plan:2ms@14826ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@14826ms
```

Focused test and whitespace checks:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  92 passed (92)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 12.45s
git diff --check produced no output
```

**Observed result after fix:** The patched CLI completed a successful local JSON agent turn, emitted final assistant JSON for `proof ok`, returned with `EXIT_CODE=0` before the timeout wrapper fired, and the survivor scan found no remaining process whose command line matched the proof session key or `model-provider-auth.worker`. The focused lifecycle test verifies the successful local JSON completion teardown invokes provider auth cleanup once.

**What was not tested:** The reporter's full Docker 10k-memory corpus and real external provider credentials were not run. The proof uses a local loopback vLLM-compatible stub to exercise the production CLI local JSON lifecycle without network credentials.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
